2. **Brutalist type at scale requires a paid license.** Söhne Breit is $400 for web. Space Grotesk is free and 85% as good. Recommend shipping the free fallback at launch and upgrading later from real revenue.
3. **Killing the cyan and amber means rewriting `globals.css` from scratch.** The current bio-cyan / amber design system is not salvageable into this direction. This is a token-level rebuild, not a recolor.
4. **The "no motion" stance loses some marketing demos.** A still page does not produce a 15-second hero video for a Twitter post. The compensating move is that the Receipt Strip is itself the video — it scrolls forever and is real.
5. **Live receipts require a real backend pipe.** This isn't dummy data. Building the receipt-emission API and the SHA-evidence pages is a 1-2 week build before the homepage can ship truthfully. Without it, the strip becomes theater and the whole direction collapses. The honest path is to ship with 5-10 real seeded receipts on day one and add velocity as the system runs.

Direction: POST-FRONTIER. White. Lime. Mono timecode. Receipt Strip. Ship it.

The Newsreader serif for editorial long-form and the §-indexed section structure (grafted from D5) deliver academic depth that signals "real research institution" not "course platform." The variable-weight reveal is a *technical* signature (real font-variation-settings axis animation, not WebGL noise) that designers will screenshot and share — the unfakeable Awwwards-tier move. Crucially, D7 preserves existing brand equity (bio-cyan #22F0D5 survives as the single live-signal accent rather than getting burned) instead of forcing a full palette reset. Light-editorial directions (D2, D4, D5, D6, D8) are correct that every AI lab is dark right now, but they each surrender either cyber gravitas (cream paper reads consulting-deck to a CISO) or the design-inspiration crown (Stripe Press is admirable but copied). D1 is the closest competitor and the cleanest Anduril-press execution, but it's so restrained it cedes the design-inspiration identity entirely.

shipBrief.summary: AtomEons V3 ships a cinematic noir editorial homepage on a near-black canvas (#08090B) with #F4F4F2 ivory type, holding bio-cyan #22F0D5 as a single live-signal accent (existing brand equity preserved, not burned) and #FF4D4D as the pulsing operational marker. Typography is Inter Variable on a 1.333 modular scale with Newsreader serif for editorial long-form and Berkeley Mono (JetBrains Mono fallback) for receipts, hashes, and timestamps — display headlines run clamp(80px,12vw,200px) at tracking -0.04em so whitespace, not type-size proliferation, carries the hierarchy. The signature move is the Variable-Weight Reveal: the lead headline animates from weight 100 to 900 and from letter-spacing 0.04em to -0.04em across the first 400px of scroll using one real Inter Variable font-variation-settings axis, paired with a persistent hairline Live Signal Panel showing build hash, install count, last commit SHA, and a 1.2s-pulsing red dot. The page is organized as a §-indexed editorial spine — Hero, § 01 The Thesis, § 02 Three Doors, § 03 Live Receipts, § 04 Current Research, § 05 Curriculum Wall, § 06 Founder Note, § 07 Colophon Footer — using full-bleed cinematic photography from /cyber-images/* and /learn-images/* (Magnum-grade B&W) so the existing 67 hero images become the content rather than waste. What is different from the current site: dark stays, the cockpit hero is replaced by a single full-bleed photograph with no Ken Burns and no above-fold CTA, the amber is retired, the bio-cyan is demoted from theme color to single live-signal accent, and the entire surface adopts a serif/mono editorial register that communicates cybersecurity gravitas, academic depth, and Awwwards-tier signature in one composition without sacrificing any of the three identities.
shipBrief.shipOrder:
1. Land design tokens first: replace app/globals.css with the V3 token file (palette #08090B/#0F1114/#F4F4F2/#9CA3AF/#5A6068/#1F242B/#22F0D5/#FF4D4D, the 1.333 modular scale 12/14/17/22/30/40/56/80/120/200, Inter Variable + Newsreader + Berkeley/JetBrains Mono font stacks, hairline 1px border tokens, --signal-pulse 1.2s keyframe). Verify next/font Inter Variable weight 100-900 axis loads in app/layout.tsx as --font-inter; add Newsreader and JetBrains Mono Variable via next/font and expose --font-serif and --font-mono. Do not delete legacy CSS variables in this pass — alias them so live pages do not regress.
2. Land the V3 component primitives under app/_components/V3/: Card.tsx (hairline-bordered container, mono caption slot, hover state at #1F242B), Header.tsx (sticky, hairline bottom border, mono nav, bio-cyan only on the live-signal pip), Footer.tsx (the § 07 Colophon — 60vh white-on-black inversion, 240px weight-900 "ÆONS", four mono columns capped at 6 items each, mono commit-hash + Marco Island coordinates hairline, no social icons, no newsletter form). Export everything through index.ts.
3. Land the homepage section components in section order under app/_components/V3/: Hero.tsx (full-bleed /cyber-images photograph, 100vh, mono eyebrow top-left, display sentence bottom-left at clamp(80px,12vw,200px) with no period, hairline Live Signal Panel bottom-right with build hash + install count + last commit SHA + pulsing #FF4D4D dot, no above-fold CTA, the Variable-Weight Reveal wired to scroll 0-400px via font-variation-settings on the headline), Pillars.tsx (§ 02 Three Doors — three stacked 100vh full-bleed photographs Cyber/Learn/Build with sticky display labels and Newsreader captions), Proof.tsx (§ 03 Live Receipts on #0F1114 with oversized mono numbers wired to real APIs — install count, weekly lines shipped, papers published, commit count, current MRR — no fake metrics), Products.tsx (§ 04 Current Research, three latest ÆoNs papers as editorial cards with Newsreader title, mono date + SHA-256, 40-word abstract first line, links to /research/<id>), Curriculum.tsx (§ 05 Curriculum Wall — dense 12-column mono index of the 67 cyber/learn images, hover reveals lesson title, click opens the lesson page), FounderQuote.tsx (§ 06 Founder Note — full-bleed workspace photograph, signed Newsreader paragraph ≤80 words, signed "— Atom McCree, Marco Island."), FinalCTA.tsx (transition strip into the Colophon Footer). Add InteriorHero.tsx for retrofitting the same hero pattern to interior pages later.
4. Wire app/page.v3.tsx as <Header/> + the seven sections in order + <Footer/>. Keep it server-rendered; mark the Variable-Weight Reveal section as a Client Component island only where scroll-linked font-variation-settings is required. Use `next/image` with priority on the hero photograph and `loading="lazy"` everywhere else; place width/height to avoid CLS.
5. Smoke-test V3 in isolation at /v3 by importing page.v3.tsx into a thin app/v3/page.tsx route. Run `pnpm dev` (or `npm run dev`), open localhost:3000/v3 desktop + mobile, capture Lighthouse, and verify: LCP ≤ 2.5s on a throttled connection, no layout shift on the Variable-Weight Reveal, prefers-reduced-motion disables scroll-linked weight animation and pulse, keyboard nav reaches every link, all text passes WCAG AA contrast on #08090B and #0F1114.
6. Promote to production: replace app/page.tsx with the contents of app/page.v3.tsx (or rename page.v3.tsx → page.tsx after archiving the existing page.tsx to page.cockpit.tsx for one cycle). Land Header and Footer as the global chrome by importing them from app/layout.tsx, replacing the legacy Header/Footer. Keep the bio-cyan accent variable so existing /orangebox and /b00kmakor product pages do not regress.
7. Verify post-promotion on Vercel preview: hit /, /orangebox, /b00kmakor, /research/* — confirm Header/Footer render, confirm interior pages still pass, confirm no broken links. Run `pnpm typecheck` and `pnpm lint`. Capture before/after screenshots desktop + mobile.
8. Sequenced retrofit pass (do not bundle into the same PR): swap the interior page hero on /cyber, /learn, /build to use V3/InteriorHero.tsx so the §-indexed editorial spine carries through the site. Defer Berkeley Mono purchase ($75) to a follow-up — JetBrains Mono Variable is the shippable fallback for v1.
9. Receipts: commit message must reference the design tokens change, the new V3 component set, and the homepage swap as three separate logical commits inside the PR. Update CHANGELOG and README homepage screenshot. Mom's Law: every section earns its place, no padding, no Apple-ad copy in body prose, no fake metrics in § 03. Atom McCree voice stays lab-grade.
